title: OfferLink Generic Interaction Strategy
summary: Architecture and implementation of framework-agnostic UI interaction for the OfferLink Chrome extension. Covers: (1) PopupDetector using MutationObserver to detect popups by position/z-index/area instead of CSS classes, (2) GenericInteraction with findByText/findNavButtons/navigateCalendar/selectOption for text-semantic-based element interaction, (3) mixed strategy where known frameworks use CSS selector fast paths and unknown frameworks use generic text-based interaction, (4) 3-step date fallback: keyboard input → popup calendar → force set, (5) comparison with agent-browser (Vercel Labs) accessibility tree approach. Also includes LLM field mapping proposal for solving the field name matching problem separately.

references.description: Conversation where generic interaction strategy was designed, compared with agent-browser, and implemented
Date picker with mixed strategy (framework fast path + generic fallback)
Select dropdown with generic PopupDetector fallback
Vercel's agent-browser project analyzed for architectural inspiration
